inputs.name: Fluid Type
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Fluid Density (rho)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Material density: CoV=0.01-0.03

inputs.name: Tank / Atmospheric Surface Pressure (P0)
inputs.hint: Open tank: 1.013 bar(a) | Closed vessel: operating pressure
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Fluid Vapor Pressure (Pv)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Static Suction Head (Hs)
inputs.hint: Positive suction head: (+) | Suction lift (pump above liquid): (-)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Suction Pipe Friction Losses (hf)
inputs.hint: Sum of friction losses from pipe runs, valves, and fittings
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Pump Required NPSH (NPSHr)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Suction Pipe Inner Diameter (D)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: API 610 Safety Margin
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Installation Altitude
inputs.hint: Sea level: 0m | Altitude decreases atmospheric pressure

formulas: Atm_Correction = 1.013 * POWER(1 - 0.0000226 * elevation_m, 5.256)   // [bar] Altitude-corrected atmospheric pressure
Head_Tank = (tank_pressure_bar * 100000) / (fluid_density_kg_m3 * 9.81)   // [m] Tank pressure head in water column equivalent
Head_Vapor = (vapor_pressure_bar * 100000) / (fluid_density_kg_m3 * 9.81)   // [m] Vapor pressure head in water column equivalent
NPSHa = Head_Tank - Head_Vapor + static_suction_head_m - suction_pipe_losses_m   // [m] Net Positive Suction Head Available (NPSHa) — API 610 Clause 6.1
NPSH_Margin_m = NPSHa - npsh_required_m   // [m] NPSH safety margin
NPSH_Ratio = NPSHa / npsh_required_m   // [—] NPSH ratio — API 610: minimum 1.1
Flow_Velocity_m_s = (flow_rate_m3h / 3600) / (3.14159 * POWER(pipe_diameter_mm / 1000 / 2, 2))   // [m/s] Suction pipe flow velocity
Max_Suction_Lift = Head_Tank - Head_Vapor - suction_pipe_losses_m - npsh_required_m - safety_margin_m   // [m] Theoretical maximum suction lift

vapor_exceeds_tank.error_msg: PHYSICAL ERROR: Vapor pressure is equal to or greater than tank pressure. Fluid is already at boiling point — pumping in liquid phase is impossible. Reduce temperature or increase tank pressure.

density_check.error_msg: Fluid density < 400 kg/m³: Not a realistic liquid (LNG is ~420, others are higher). Verify input value.

suction_lift_max.error_msg: Suction lift < -9m: Theoretical maximum vacuum lift for water is ~9m. This value exceeds physical limits. Revise pump position.

pipe_velocity_check.error_msg: Suction line velocity > 3 m/s: Friction losses will be excessive, reducing NPSHa and increasing cavitation risk. Hydraulic Institute recommendation: suction velocity < 1.5 m/s.

smart_warnings.message: CAVITATION INITIATED: NPSHa < NPSHr. Vapor bubbles are forming in the impeller eye. Micro-implosions are causing surface erosion. Stop the pump immediately and rectify suction conditions.

CAVITATION RISK: Does not meet API 610 requirement of NPSHa >= NPSHr + 10% safety margin. Cavitation will initiate immediately on flow surges or temperature rises. Reduce suction line friction losses.
Insufficient NPSH margin: NPSHa / NPSHr is between 1.1 and 1.3. Cavitation may trigger during process fluctuations (temperature, flow). Hydraulic Institute recommends a minimum ratio of 1.3.
Hot fluid + negative suction lift: Vapor pressure increases exponentially with temperature, rapidly reducing NPSHa. This combination poses critical cavitation risk. Locate pump below tank level.
Suction line friction losses > 2m: Directly subtracts from NPSHa. To reduce losses: increase pipe diameter, minimize valves, shorten pipe runs, and avoid tight elbows.
NPSHa < 1.0m: Operating conditions are outside normal centrifugal pump limits. Evaluate special low-NPSH pump models (with inducer) or submersible pumps.

fmea.effect: PHYSICAL ERROR: Vapor pressure is equal to or greater than tank pressure. Fluid is already at boiling point — pumping in liquid phase is impossible. Reduce temperature or increase tank pressure.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Fluid density < 400 kg/m³: Not a realistic liquid (LNG is ~420, others are higher). Verify input value.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Suction lift < -9m: Theoretical maximum vacuum lift for water is ~9m. This value exceeds physical limits. Revise pump position.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Suction line velocity > 3 m/s: Friction losses will be excessive, reducing NPSHa and increasing cavitation risk. Hydraulic Institute recommendation: suction velocity < 1.5 m/s.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: CAVITATION INITIATED: NPSHa < NPSHr. Vapor bubbles are forming in the impeller eye. Micro-implosions are causing surface erosion. Stop the pump immediately and rectify suction conditions.

CAVITATION RISK: Does not meet API 610 requirement of NPSHa >= NPSHr + 10% safety margin. Cavitation will initiate immediately on flow surges or temperature rises. Reduce suction line friction losses.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: NPSH_Ratio >= 1.0 && NPSH_Ratio < 1.1

fmea.failureMode: Insufficient NPSH margin: NPSHa / NPSHr is between 1.1 and 1.3. Cavitation may trigger during process fluctuations (temperature, flow). Hydraulic Institute recommends a minimum ratio of 1.3.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: NPSH_Ratio >= 1.1 && NPSH_Ratio < 1.3

fmea.failureMode: Hot fluid + negative suction lift: Vapor pressure increases exponentially with temperature, rapidly reducing NPSHa. This combination poses critical cavitation risk. Locate pump below tank level.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fluid_temp_c > 60 && static_suction_head_m < 0

fmea.failureMode: Suction line friction losses > 2m: Directly subtracts from NPSHa. To reduce losses: increase pipe diameter, minimize valves, shorten pipe runs, and avoid tight elbows.
NPSHa < 1.0m: Operating conditions are outside normal centrifugal pump limits. Evaluate special low-NPSH pump models (with inducer) or submersible pumps.
